displayTitle: How Deep Can the Probe See
kicker: ET LEVEL I // FREQUENCY, PHASE & DEPTH

beats.narration: Eddy currents never fill a part evenly. They crowd the surface and fade with depth, and how deep your probe sees is computable.

payload.title: DEPTH OF PENETRATION
payload.sub: The skin effect, as one solvable number.

beats.narration: The fade is exponential, measured in standard depths. At one standard depth, current density is thirty-seven percent of the surface; by three, just five.

payload.heading: The current fades by the numbers

beats.narration: Beyond three standard depths, too little current crosses a flaw to disturb the coil. Three standard depths is the accepted effective depth of detection.
Depth follows one over the square root of frequency, conductivity, and permeability. Frequency is the only term you control, and quadrupling it halves the depth.

payload.heading: Frequency is the one knob you turn

beats.narration: So the call is simple. Raise the frequency for surface cracks, lower it to reach second layers, and verify every choice on your reference standard.

payload.sub: Gain cannot create current where none flows.
